title: Canto 10 Chapter 79
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचतत: पर्वण्युपावृत्ते प्रचण्ड: पांशुवर्षण: ।भीमो वायुरभूद् राजन्पूयगन्धस्तु सर्वश: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatataḥ parvaṇy upāvṛttepracaṇḍaḥ pāṁśu-varṣaṇaḥbhīmo vāyur abhūd rājanpūya-gandhas tu sarvaśaḥ
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Then, on the new-moon day, O King, a fierce and frightening wind arose, scattering dust all about and spreading the smell of pus everywhere.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; tataḥ; parvaṇi; upāvṛtte; pracaṇḍaḥ; pāṁśu; varṣaṇaḥ; bhīmaḥ; vāyuḥ; abhūt; rājan; pūya; gandhaḥ; tu; sarvaśaḥ

verses.text: ततोऽमेध्यमयं वर्षं बल्वलेन विनिर्मितम् ।अभवद् यज्ञशालायां सोऽन्वद‍ृश्यत शूलधृक् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’medhya-mayaṁ varṣaṁbalvalena vinirmitamabhavad yajña-śālāyāṁso ’nvadṛśyata śūla-dhṛk
verses.meaning: Next, onto the sacrificial arena came a downpour of abominable things sent by Balvala, after which the demon himself appeared, trident in hand.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; amedhya; mayam; varṣam; balvalena; vinirmitam; abhavat; yajña; śālāyām; saḥ; anvadṛśyata; śūla; dhṛk

verses.text: तं विलोक्य बृहत्कायं भिन्नाञ्जनचयोपमम् ।तप्तताम्रशिखाश्मश्रुं दंष्ट्रोग्रभ्रुकुटीमुखम् ॥ ३ ॥सस्मार मूषलं राम: परसैन्यविदारणम् ।हलं च दैत्यदमनं ते तूर्णमुपतस्थतु: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vilokya bṛhat-kāyaṁbhinnāñjana-cayopamamtapta-tāmra-śikhā-śmaśruṁdaṁṣṭrogra-bhru-kuṭī-mukham
verses.meaning: The immense demon resembled a mass of black carbon. His topknot and beard were like molten copper, and his face had horrible fangs and furrowed eyebrows. Upon seeing him, Lord Balarāma thought of His club, which tears to pieces His enemies’ armies, and His plow weapon, which punishes the demons. Thus summoned, His two weapons appeared before Him at once.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; vilokya; bṛhat; kāyam; bhinna; añjana; caya; upamam; tapta; tāmra; śikhā; śmaśrum; daṁṣṭrā; ugra; bhru; kuṭī; mukham; sasmāra; mūṣalam; rāmaḥ; para; sainya; vidāraṇam; halam; ca; daitya; damanam; te; tūrṇam; upatasthatuḥ

verses.text: तमाकृष्य हलाग्रेण बल्वलं गगनेचरम् ।मूषलेनाहनत्क्रुद्धो मूर्ध्‍नि ब्रह्मद्रुहं बल: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam ākṛṣya halāgreṇabalvalaṁ gagane-carammūṣalenāhanat kruddhomūrdhni brahma-druhaṁ balaḥ
verses.meaning: With the tip of His plow Lord Balarāma caught hold of the demon Balvala as he flew through the sky, and with His club the Lord angrily struck that harasser of brāhmaṇas on the head.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; ākṛṣya; hala; agreṇa; balvalam; gagane; caram; mūṣalena; ahanat; kruddhaḥ; mūrdhni; brahma; druham; balaḥ

verses.text: सोऽपतद्भ‍ुवि निर्भिन्नललाटोऽसृक् समुत्सृजन् ।मुञ्चन्नार्तस्वरं शैलो यथा वज्रहतोऽरुण: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’patad bhuvi nirbhinna-lalāṭo ’sṛk samutsṛjanmuñcann ārta-svaraṁ śailoyathā vajra-hato ’ruṇaḥ
verses.meaning: Balvala cried out in agony and fell to the ground, his forehead cracked open and gushing blood. He resembled a red mountain struck by a lightning bolt.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; apatat; bhuvi; nirbhinna; lalāṭaḥ; asṛk; samutsṛjan; muñcan; ārta; svaram; śailaḥ; yathā; vajra; hataḥ; aruṇaḥ
verses.purport: According to the ācāryas, the demon appeared reddish with blood, like a mountain red with oxide.

verses.text: संस्तुत्य मुनयो रामं प्रयुज्यावितथाशिष: ।अभ्यषिञ्चन् महाभागा वृत्रघ्नं विबुधा यथा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁstutya munayo rāmaṁprayujyāvitathāśiṣaḥabhyaṣiñcan mahā-bhāgāvṛtra-ghnaṁ vibudhā yathā
verses.meaning: The exalted sages honored Lord Rāma with sincere prayers and awarded Him infallible blessings. Then they performed His ritual bath, just as the demigods had formally bathed Indra when he killed Vṛtra.
verses.wordMeanings: saṁstutya; munayaḥ; rāmam; prayujya; avitatha; āśiṣaḥ; abhyaṣiñcan; mahā; bhāgāḥ; vṛtra; ghnam; vibudhāḥ; yathā

verses.text: वैजयन्तीं ददुर्मालां श्रीधामाम्‍लानपङ्कजाम् ।रामाय वाससी दिव्ये दिव्यान्याभरणानि च ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaijayantīṁ dadur mālāṁśrī-dhāmāmlāna-paṅkajāṁrāmāya vāsasī divyedivyāny ābharaṇāni ca
verses.meaning: They gave Lord Balarāma a Vaijayantī garland of unfading lotuses in which resided the goddess of fortune, and they also gave Him a set of divine garments and jewelry.
verses.wordMeanings: vaijayantīm; daduḥ; mālām; śrī; dhāma; amlāna; paṅkajām; rāmāya; vāsasī; divye; divyāni; ābharaṇāni; ca

verses.text: अथ तैरभ्यनुज्ञात: कौशिकीमेत्य ब्राह्मणै: ।स्‍नात्वा सरोवरमगाद् यत: सरयूरास्रवत् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha tair abhyanujñātaḥkauśikīm etya brāhmaṇaiḥsnātvā sarovaram agādyataḥ sarayūr āsravat
verses.meaning: Then, given leave by the sages, the Lord went with a contingent of brāhmaṇas to the Kauśikī River, where He bathed. From there He went to the lake from which flows the river Sarayū.
verses.wordMeanings: atha; taiḥ; abhyanujñātaḥ; kauśikīm; etya; brāhmaṇaiḥ; snātvā; sarovaram; agāt; yataḥ; sarayūḥ; āsravat

verses.text: अनुस्रोतेन सरयूं प्रयागमुपगम्य स: ।स्‍नात्वा सन्तर्प्य देवादीन्जगाम पुलहाश्रमम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: anu-srotena sarayūṁprayāgam upagamya saḥsnātvā santarpya devādīnjagāma pulahāśramam
verses.meaning: The Lord followed the course of the Sarayū until He came to Prayāga, where He bathed and then performed rituals to propitiate the demigods and other living beings. Next He went to the āśrama of Pulaha Ṛṣi.
verses.wordMeanings: anu; srotena; sarayūm; prayāgam; upagamya; saḥ; snātvā; santarpya; deva; ādīn; jagāma; pulaha; āśramam
verses.purport: Pulahāśrama is also known as Hari-kṣetra.

verses.text: गोमतीं गण्डकीं स्‍नात्वा विपाशां शोण आप्लुत: ।गयां गत्वा पितृनिष्ट्वा गङ्गासागरसङ्गमे ॥ ११ ॥उपस्पृश्य महेन्द्राद्रौ रामं द‍ृष्ट्वाभिवाद्य च ।सप्तगोदावरीं वेणां पम्पां भीमरथीं तत: ॥ १२ ॥स्कन्दं द‍ृष्ट्वा ययौ राम: श्रीशैलं गिरिशालयम् ।द्रविडेषु महापुण्यं द‍ृष्ट्वाद्रिं वेङ्कटं प्रभु: ॥ १३ ॥कामकोष्णीं पुरीं काञ्चीं कावेरीं च सरिद्वराम् ।श्रीरङ्गाख्यं महापुण्यं यत्र सन्निहितो हरि: ॥ १४ ॥ऋषभाद्रिं हरे: क्षेत्रं दक्षिणां मथुरां तथा ।सामुद्रं सेतुमगमत्महापातकनाशनम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gomatīṁ gaṇḍakīṁ snātvāvipāśāṁ śoṇa āplutaḥgayāṁ gatvā pitṝn iṣṭvāgaṅgā-sāgara-saṅgame
verses.meaning: Lord Balarāma bathed in the Gomatī, Gaṇḍakī and Vipāśā rivers, and also immersed Himself in the Śoṇa. He went to Gayā, where He worshiped His forefathers, and to the mouth of the Ganges, where He performed purifying ablutions. At Mount Mahendra He saw Lord Paraśurāma and offered Him prayers, and then He bathed in the seven branches of the Godāvarī River, and also in the rivers Veṇā, Pampā and Bhīmarathī. Then Lord Balarāma met Lord Skanda and visited Śrī-śaila, the abode of Lord Giriśa. In the southern provinces known as Draviḍa-deśa the Supreme Lord saw the sacred Veṅkaṭa Hill, as well as the cities of Kāmakoṣṇī and Kāñcī, the exalted Kāverī River and the most holy Śrī-raṅga, where Lord Kṛṣṇa has manifested Himself. From there He went to Ṛṣabha Mountain, where Lord Kṛṣṇa also lives, and to the southern Mathurā. Then He came to Setubandha, where the most grievous sins are destroyed.
verses.wordMeanings: gomatīm; gaṇḍakīm; snātvā; vipāśām; śoṇe; āplutaḥ; gayām; gatvā; pitṝn; iṣṭvā; gaṅgā; sāgara; saṅgame; upaspṛśya; mahā; indra; adrau; rāmam; dṛṣṭvā; abhivādya; ca; sapta; godāvarīm; veṇām; pampām; bhīmarathīm; tataḥ; skandam; dṛṣṭvā; yayau; rāmaḥ; śrī; śailam; giri; śa; ālayam; draviḍeṣu; mahā; puṇyam; dṛṣṭvā; adrim; veṅkaṭam; prabhuḥ; kāma; koṣṇīm; purīm; kāñcīm; kāverīm; ca; sarit; varām; śrī; raṅga; ākhyam; mahā; puṇyam; yatra; sannihitaḥ; hariḥ; ṛṣabha; adrim; hareḥ; kṣetram; dakṣiṇām; mathurām; tathā; sāmudram; setum; agamat; mahā; pātaka; nāśanam
verses.purport: Usually one goes to Gayā to worship deceased forefathers. But as Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains, although Lord Balarāma’s father and grandfather were still alive, it was on His father’s order that He carefully worshiped His forefathers at Gayā. Drawing insight from the Vaiṣṇava-toṣaṇī, the ācārya further explains that although Lord Balarāma was in the immediate proximity of Jagannātha Purī, He did not go there, since He wanted to avoid the embarrassment of having to worship Himself among the forms of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Balabhadra and Subhadrā.

verses.text: तत्रायुतमदाद् धेनूर्ब्राह्मणेभ्यो हलायुध: ।कृतमालां ताम्रपर्णीं मलयं च कुलाचलम् ॥ १६ ॥तत्रागस्त्यं समासीनं नमस्कृत्याभिवाद्य च ।योजितस्तेन चाशीर्भिरनुज्ञातो गतोऽर्णवम् ।दक्षिणं तत्र कन्याख्यां दुर्गां देवीं ददर्श स: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāyutam adād dhenūrbrāhmaṇebhyo halāyudhaḥkṛtamālāṁ tāmraparṇīṁmalayaṁ ca kulācalam
verses.meaning: There at Setubandha [Rāmeśvaram] Lord Halāyudha gave brāhmaṇas ten thousand cows in charity. He then visited the Kṛtamālā and Tāmraparṇī rivers and the great Malaya Mountains. In the Malaya range Lord Balarāma found Agastya Ṛṣi sitting in meditation. After bowing down to the sage, the Lord offered him prayers and then received blessings from him. Taking leave from Agastya, He proceeded to the shore of the southern ocean, where He saw Goddess Durgā in her form of Kanyā-kumārī.
verses.wordMeanings: tatra; ayutam; adāt; dhenūḥ; brahmaṇebhyaḥ; hala; āyudhaḥ; kṛtamālām; tāmraparṇīm; malayam; ca; kula; acalam; tatra; agastyam; samāsīnam; namaskṛtya; abhivādya; ca; yojitaḥ; tena; ca; āśīrbhiḥ; anujñātaḥ; gataḥ; arṇavam; dakṣiṇam; tatra; kanyā; ākhyām; durgām; devīm; dadarśa; saḥ

verses.text: तत: फाल्गुनमासाद्य पञ्चाप्सरसमुत्तमम् ।विष्णु: सन्निहितो यत्र स्‍नात्वास्पर्शद् गवायुतम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ phālgunam āsādyapañcāpsarasam uttamamviṣṇuḥ sannihito yatrasnātvāsparśad gavāyutam
verses.meaning: Next He went to Phālguna-tīrtha and bathed in the sacred Pañcāpsarā Lake, where Lord Viṣṇu had directly manifested Himself. At this place He gave away another ten thousand cows.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; phālgunam; āsādya; pañca; apsarasam; uttamam; viṣṇuḥ; sannihitaḥ; yatra; snātvā; asparśat; gava; ayutam

verses.text: ततोऽभिव्रज्य भगवान् केरलांस्तु त्रिगर्तकान् ।गोकर्णाख्यं शिवक्षेत्रं सान्निध्यं यत्र धूर्जटे: ॥ १९ ॥आर्यां द्वैपायनीं द‍ृष्ट्वा शूर्पारकमगाद् बल: ।तापीं पयोष्णीं निर्विन्ध्यामुपस्पृश्याथ दण्डकम् ॥ २० ॥प्रविश्य रेवामगमद् यत्र माहिष्मती पुरी ।मनुतीर्थमुपस्पृश्य प्रभासं पुनरागमत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’bhivrajya bhagavānkeralāṁs tu trigartakāngokarṇākhyaṁ śiva-kṣetraṁsānnidhyaṁ yatra dhūrjaṭeḥ
verses.meaning: The Supreme Lord then traveled through the kingdoms of Kerala and Trigarta, visiting Lord Śiva’s sacred city of Gokarṇa, where Lord Dhūrjaṭi [Śiva] directly manifests himself. After also visiting Goddess Pārvatī, who dwells on an island, Lord Balarāma went to the holy district of Śūrpāraka and bathed in the Tāpī, Payoṣṇī and Nirvindhyā rivers. He next entered the Daṇḍaka forest and went to the river Revā, along which the city of Māhiṣmatī is found. Then He bathed at Manu-tīrtha and finally returned to Prabhāsa.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; abhivrajya; bhagavān; keralān; tu; trigartakān; gokarṇa; ākhyam; śiva; kṣetram; sānnidhyam; yatra; dhūrjaṭeḥ; āryām; dvaipa; ayanīm; dṛṣṭvā; śūrpārakam; agāt; balaḥ; tāpīm; payoṣṇīm; nirvindhyām; upaspṛśya; atha; daṇḍakam; praviśya; revām; agamat; yatra; māhiṣmatī; purī; manu; tīrtham; upaspṛśya; prabhāsam; punaḥ; āgamat

verses.text: श्रुत्वा द्विजै: कथ्यमानं कुरुपाण्डवसंयुगे ।सर्वराजन्यनिधनं भारं मेने हृतं भुव: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā dvijaiḥ kathyamānaṁkuru-pāṇḍava-saṁyugesarva-rājanya-nidhanaṁbhāraṁ mene hṛtaṁ bhuvaḥ
verses.meaning: The Lord heard from some brāhmaṇas how all the kings involved in the battle between the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas had been killed. From this He concluded that the earth was now relieved of her burden.
verses.wordMeanings: śrutvā; dvijaiḥ; kathyamānam; kuru; pāṇḍava; saṁyuge; sarva; rājanya; nidhanam; bhāram; mene; hṛtam; bhuvaḥ

verses.text: स भीमदुर्योधनयोर्गदाभ्यां युध्यतोर्मृधे ।वारयिष्यन् विनशनं जगाम यदुनन्दन: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa bhīma-duryodhanayorgadābhyāṁ yudhyator mṛdhevārayiṣyan vinaśanaṁjagāma yadu-nandanaḥ
verses.meaning: Wanting to stop the club fight then raging between Bhīma and Duryodhana on the battlefield, Lord Balarāma went to Kurukṣetra.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; bhīma; duryodhanayoḥ; gadābhyām; yudhyatoḥ; mṛdhe; vārayiṣyan; vinaśanam; jagāma; yadu; nandanaḥ

verses.text: युधिष्ठिरस्तु तं द‍ृष्ट्वा यमौ कृष्णार्जुनावपि ।अभिवाद्याभवंस्तुष्णीं किं विवक्षुरिहागत: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiras tu taṁ dṛṣṭvāyamau kṛṣṇārjunāv apiabhivādyābhavaṁs tuṣṇīṁkiṁ vivakṣur ihāgataḥ
verses.meaning: When Yudhiṣṭhira, Lord Kṛṣṇa, Arjuna and the twin brothers Nakula and Sahadeva saw Lord Balarāma, they offered Him respectful obeisances but said nothing, thinking “What has He come here to tell us?”
verses.wordMeanings: yudhiṣṭhiraḥ; tu; tam; dṛṣṭvā; yamau; kṛṣṇa; arjunau; api; abhivādya; abhavan; tuṣṇīm; kim; vivakṣuḥ; iha; āgataḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Prabhupāda writes: “The reason they were silent was that Lord Balarāma was somewhat affectionate toward Duryodhana, who had learned from Balarāmajī the art of fighting with a club. Thus, when the fighting was going on, King Yudhiṣṭhira and the others thought that Balarāma might have come there to say something in favor of Duryodhana, and therefore they remained silent.”

verses.text: गदापाणी उभौ द‍ृष्ट्वा संरब्धौ विजयैषिणौ ।मण्डलानि विचित्राणि चरन्ताविदमब्रवीत् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gadā-pāṇī ubhau dṛṣṭvāsaṁrabdhau vijayaiṣiṇaumaṇḍalāni vicitrāṇicarantāv idam abravīt
verses.meaning: Lord Balarāma found Duryodhana and Bhīma with clubs in their hands, each furiously striving for victory over the other as they circled about skillfully. The Lord addressed them as follows.
verses.wordMeanings: gadā; pāṇī; ubhau; dṛṣṭvā; saṁrabdhau; vijaya; eṣiṇau; maṇḍalāni; vicitrāṇi; carantau; idam; abravīt

verses.text: युवां तुल्यबलौ वीरौ हे राजन् हे वृकोदर ।एकं प्राणाधिकं मन्ये उतैकं शिक्षयाधिकम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yuvāṁ tulya-balau vīrauhe rājan he vṛkodaraekaṁ prāṇādhikaṁ manyeutaikaṁ śikṣayādhikam
verses.meaning: [Lord Balarāma said:] King Duryodhana! And Bhīma! Listen! You two warriors are equal in fighting prowess. I know that one of you has greater physical power, while the other is better trained in technique.
verses.wordMeanings: yuvām; tulya; balau; vīrau; he; rājan; he; vṛkodara; ekam; prāṇa; adhikam; manye; uta; ekam; śikṣayā; adhikam
verses.purport: Bhīma was physically more powerful, but Duryodhana was more advanced in terms of technique.

verses.text: तस्मादेकतरस्येह युवयो: समवीर्ययो: ।न लक्ष्यते जयोऽन्यो वा विरमत्वफलो रण: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād ekatarasyehayuvayoḥ sama-vīryayoḥna lakṣyate jayo ’nyo vāviramatv aphalo raṇaḥ
verses.meaning: Since you are so evenly matched in fighting prowess, I do not see how either of you can win or lose this duel. Therefore please stop this useless battle.
verses.wordMeanings: tasmāt; ekatarasya; iha; yuvayoḥ; sama; vīryayoḥ; na; lakṣyate; jayaḥ; anyaḥ; vā; viramatu; aphalaḥ; raṇaḥ

verses.text: न तद्वाक्यं जगृहतुर्बद्धवैरौ नृपार्थवत् ।अनुस्मरन्तावन्योन्यं दुरुक्तं दुष्कृतानि च ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: na tad-vākyaṁ jagṛhaturbaddha-vairau nṛpārthavatanusmarantāv anyonyaṁduruktaṁ duṣkṛtāni ca
verses.meaning: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] They did not accept Lord Balarāma’s request, O King, although it was logical, for their mutual enmity was irrevocable. Each of them constantly remembered the insults and injuries he had suffered from the other.
verses.wordMeanings: na; tat; vākyam; jagṛhatuḥ; baddha; vairau; nṛpa; artha; vat; anusmarantau; anyonyam; duruktam; duṣkṛtāni; ca

verses.text: दिष्टं तदनुमन्वानो रामो द्वारवतीं ययौ ।उग्रसेनादिभि: प्रीतैर्ज्ञातिभि: समुपागत: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: diṣṭaṁ tad anumanvānorāmo dvāravatīṁ yayauugrasenādibhiḥ prītairjñātibhiḥ samupāgataḥ
verses.meaning: Concluding that the battle was the arrangement of fate, Lord Balarāma went back to Dvārakā. There He was greeted by Ugrasena and His other relatives, who were all delighted to see Him.
verses.wordMeanings: diṣṭam; tat; anumanvānaḥ; rāmaḥ; dvāravatīm; yayau; ugrasena; ādibhiḥ; prītaiḥ; jñātibhiḥ; samupāgataḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains that the word diṣṭam, “fate,” indicates that the battle between Bhīma and Duryodhana had been enjoined by Lord Kṛṣṇa and set into motion by Him.

verses.text: तं पुनर्नैमिषं प्राप्तमृषयोऽयाजयन् मुदा ।क्रत्वङ्गं क्रतुभि: सर्वैर्निवृत्ताखिलविग्रहम् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ punar naimiṣaṁ prāptamṛṣayo ’yājayan mudākratv-aṅgaṁ kratubhiḥ sarvairnivṛttākhila-vigraham
verses.meaning: Later Lord Balarāma returned to Naimiṣāraṇya, where the sages joyfully engaged Him, the embodiment of all sacrifice, in performing various kinds of Vedic sacrifice. Lord Balarāma was now retired from warfare.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; punaḥ; naimiṣam; prāptam; ṛṣayaḥ; ayājayan; mudā; kratu; aṅgam; kratubhiḥ; sarvaiḥ; nivṛtta; akhila; vigraham
verses.purport: Śrīla Prabhupāda writes: “[When Lord Balarāma] went to the holy place of pilgrimage at Naimiṣāraṇya,… the sages, saintly persons and brāhmaṇas all stood up to receive Him. They understood that Lord Balarāma, although a kṣatriya, was now retired from the fighting business. The brāhmaṇas and the sages, who were always for peace and tranquillity, were very pleased at this. All of them embraced Balarāma with great affection and induced Him to perform various kinds of sacrifices in that sacred spot of Naimiṣāraṇya. Actually Lord Balarāma had no business performing the sacrifices recommended for ordinary human beings; He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and therefore He Himself is the enjoyer of all such sacrifices. As such, His exemplary action in performing sacrifices was only to give a lesson to the common man to show how one should abide by the injunctions of the Vedas.”

verses.text: तेभ्यो विशुद्धं विज्ञानं भगवान् व्यतरद् विभु: ।येनैवात्मन्यदो विश्वमात्मानं विश्वगं विदु: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tebhyo viśuddhaṁ vijñānaṁbhagavān vyatarad vibhuḥyenaivātmany ado viśvamātmānaṁ viśva-gaṁ viduḥ
verses.meaning: The all-powerful Lord Balarāma bestowed upon the sages pure spiritual knowledge, by which they could see the whole universe within Him and also see Him pervading everything.
verses.wordMeanings: tebhyaḥ; viśuddham; vijñānam; bhagavān; vyatarat; vibhuḥ; yena; eva; ātmani; adaḥ; viśvam; ātmānam; viśva; gam; viduḥ

verses.text: स्वपत्यावभृथस्‍नातो ज्ञातिबन्धुसुहृद् वृत: ।रेजे स्वज्योत्स्‍नयेवेन्दु: सुवासा: सुष्ठ्वलङ्कृत: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-patyāvabhṛtha-snātojñāti-bandhu-suhṛd-vṛtaḥreje sva-jyotsnayevenduḥsu-vāsāḥ suṣṭhv alaṅkṛtaḥ
verses.meaning: After executing with His wife the avabhṛtha ablutions, the beautifully dressed and ornamented Lord Balarāma, encircled by His immediate family and other relatives and friends, looked as splendid as the moon surrounded by its effulgent rays.
verses.wordMeanings: sva; patyā; avabhṛtha; snātaḥ; jñāti; bandhu; suhṛt; vṛtaḥ; reje; sva; jyotsnayā; iva; induḥ; su; vāsāḥ; suṣṭhu; alaṅkṛtaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Prabhupāda beautifully describes this scene as follows: “Lord Balarāma then took the avabhṛtha bath, which is taken after finishing sacrificial performances. After taking His bath, He dressed Himself in new silken garments and decorated Himself with beautiful jewelry. Amidst His relatives and friends, He appeared to be a shining full moon amidst the luminaries in the sky.”

verses.text: ईद‍ृग्विधान्यसङ्ख्यानि बलस्य बलशालिन: ।अनन्तस्याप्रमेयस्य मायामर्त्यस्य सन्ति हि ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: īdṛg-vidhāny asaṅkhyānibalasya bala-śālinaḥanantasyāprameyasyamāyā-martyasya santi hi
verses.meaning: Countless other such pastimes were performed by mighty Balarāma, the unlimited and immeasurable Supreme Lord, whose mystic Yoga-māyā power makes Him appear to be a human being.
verses.wordMeanings: īdṛk; vidhāni; asaṅkhyāni; balasya; bala; śālinaḥ; anantasya; aprameyasya; māyā; martyasya; santi; hi

verses.text: योऽनुस्मरेत रामस्य कर्माण्यद्भ‍ुतकर्मण: ।सायं प्रातरनन्तस्य विष्णो: स दयितो भवेत् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yo ’nusmareta rāmasyakarmāṇy adbhuta-karmaṇaḥsāyaṁ prātar anantasyaviṣṇoḥ sa dayito bhavet
verses.meaning: All the activities of the unlimited Lord Balarāma are amazing. Anyone who regularly remembers them at dawn and dusk will become very dear to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Śrī Viṣṇu.
verses.wordMeanings: yaḥ; anusmareta; rāmasya; karmāṇi; adbhuta; karmaṇaḥ; sāyam; prātaḥ; anantasya; viṣṇoḥ; saḥ; dayitaḥ; bhavet
verses.purport: Śrīla Prabhupāda writes: “Lord Balarāma is the original Viṣṇu; therefore anyone remembering these pastimes of Lord Balarāma in the morning and evening will certainly become a great devotee of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and thus his life will become successful in all respects.”
